messages.content:
What is the aim of the European Union’s Sustainable Financial Disclosure Regulation?


It is aimed at ensuring transparency in the sourcing of raw materials and managing relationships in the value chain.

It is aimed at ensuring that a company has a strong environmental agenda and becomes an environmentally responsible organization.

It is aimed at ensuring asset managers, financial advisors, and other financial market participants consider sustainability and environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors in their decision-making around investments.

It is aimed at obtaining transparent, traceable, and quality environmental, social, and governance (ESG) data from companies across their value chains.

The correct answer is: It is aimed at ensuring asset managers, financial advisors, and other financial market participants consider sustainability and environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors in their decision-making around investments.

Aercrux Corporation Pvt. Ltd. has decided to embrace the opportunities of a responsible value chain along the three pillars of Trust, Net Zero, and Circular. Which of the following actions can the company take toward achieving Net Zero?


Provide a living wage or income to everyone who directly provides goods and services to the company.

Transition to 100% renewable electricity in production and become carbon negative for all their direct and indirect emissions.

Make their materials packaging returnable or recyclable.
Transition to 100% renewable electricity in production and become carbon negative for all their direct and indirect emissions.

Identify the key drivers that enable businesses to win stakeholder trust in a responsible value chain.


Ethical sourcing of raw materials and supply chain traceability

Fair wages and health and safety for all workers

Responsible treatment of hazardous substances and supply chain traceability

Supply chain traceability, supplier ethics and transparency, working conditions including fair wages and health and safety, and zero corruption

The key drivers that enable businesses to win stakeholder trust in a responsible value chain are:

Supply chain traceability, supplier ethics and transparency, working conditions including fair wages and health and safety, and zero corruption.

Which of the following are the three most important areas that comprise a responsible value chain?


Trust, Net Zero, Leadership

Trust, Use Extension, Sustainable

Trust, Net Zero, Circular

Net Zero, Circular, Use Extension
Trust, Net Zero, Circular

Accenture has set ambitious sustainability goals such as recycling 100% of its waste and significantly reducing water usage. The company plans to achieve net zero by _______.


2030

2050

2025

2040
The correct answer is: 2030

Intelligent solutions through digital transformation drive results from every aspect of the responsible value chain. Which of the following is an example of how intelligent solutions drive impact at the design stage?


Leveraging the digital twin technology to help clients optimize design to build products with low carbon footprint

Digitizing the research and development processes

Introducing product platform engineering and modernization
Leveraging the digital twin technology to help clients optimize design to build products with low carbon footprint

Match each scope of emission against its appropriate source.


1.
Scope 1

 
Includes indirect emissions released from all the other activities in which a business is engaged

2.
Scope 3

 
Includes direct emissions that are caused due to business activities

3.
Scope 2

 
Includes indirect emissions that come from the electricity purchased by the company

1. Scope 2 - Includes indirect emissions that come from the electricity purchased by the company
2. Scope 1 - Includes direct emissions that are caused due to business activities
3. Scope 3 - Includes indirect emissions released from all the other activities in which a business is engaged

Identify the five important elements that businesses need to examine in any ambition-setting exercise.


Capabilities; governance; operating models; systems; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives

Capabilities; governance; technology, tools, and insights; roles and responsibilities; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives

Capabilities; governance; technology, tools, and insights; operating models; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives

Capabilities; processes; technology, tools, and insights; roles and responsibilities; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives
Capabilities; governance; technology, tools, and insights; roles and responsibilities; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives

Match each stakeholder group to their expectations from business regarding sustainability.


1.
Consumers

 
They are willing to work for organizations with strong environmental commitments.

2.
Investors

 
They consider the integration of extra-financial key performance indicators (KPIs) such as environmental, social, and governance (ESG) ratings of businesses while making investment decisions.

3.
Employees

 
They believe companies should take a stand on important social, environmental, political, or cultural issues

1. Consumers - They are willing to work for organizations with strong environmental commitments.
2. Investors - They consider the integration of extra-financial key performance indicators (KPIs) such as environmental, social, and governance (ESG) ratings of businesses while making investment decisions.
3. Employees - They believe companies should take a stand on important social, environmental, political, or cultural issues.

How It Combines with Other SQ Topics and 360 Value Describe how green software aligns with Accenture’s overall sustainability agenda.


1.
Financial

 
By enabling sustainability in technology, green software establishes a strong correlation between sustainability and profitability.

2.
Sustainability

 
Green software makes it possible to provide technologies such as online collaboration and video conferencing to maintain quality customer experience and keep employees engaged.

3.
Experience

 
The focus is on environmental value, given that green software contributes to the environmental aspect of environmental, social, and governance (ESG) by reducing carbon emissions across the organization’s IT stack

1. Financial - By enabling sustainability in technology, green software establishes a strong correlation between sustainability and profitability.
2. Experience - Green software makes it possible to provide technologies such as online collaboration and video conferencing to maintain quality customer experience and keep employees engaged.
3. Sustainability - The focus is on environmental value, given that green software contributes to the environmental aspect of environmental, social, and governance (ESG) by reducing carbon emissions across the organization’s IT stack.

Identify the key areas of the 360° Value framework in which green software delivers impact.


Experience, Custom, Talent

Financial, Custom, Inclusion and Diversity

Sustainability, Inclusion and Diversity, Talent

Sustainability, Financial, Experience
Sustainability, Financial, Experience

Which of the following is a way to make software more green?


Adjusting the software development life cycle to be leaner and cleaner

Using software for greener use such as tracking deforestation

Making software more secure

Making efficient use of human resources
Adjusting the software development life cycle to be leaner and cleaner

WIdentify the three important areas that organizations can consider to successfully transform to a green software mindset.


Green processes, green cloud, and green operating models

Green IT infrastructure, green operating models, and green technology and tools

Green IT infrastructure, green cloud, and green software

Green software, green capabilities, and green governance
Green IT infrastructure, green operating models, and green technology and tools

Identify the three important areas that organizations can consider to successfully transform to a green software mindset.


Green processes, green cloud, and green operating models

Green IT infrastructure, green operating models, and green technology and tools

Green IT infrastructure, green cloud, and green software

Green software, green capabilities, and green governance
Green IT infrastructure, green operating models, and green technology and tools

Identify the three important areas that organizations can consider to successfully transform to a green software mindset.


Green processes, green cloud, and green operating models

Green IT infrastructure, green operating models, and green technology and tools

Green IT infrastructure, green cloud, and green software

Green software, green capabilities, and green governance
Green IT infrastructure, green operating models, and green technology and tools
